title: Tables & Forms
summary: Build tables for data and forms for user input — text fields, choices, dropdowns, and submit buttons.

examples.title: Table rows and cells: tr, th, td
examples.description: <tr> is a table row, <th> is a header cell, and <td> is a data cell. Display grades, schedules, or any data in rows and columns. Add a third student row to the table.

examples.title: Table border, padding, and spacing
examples.description: CSS controls how tables look — border-collapse removes gaps between cells, padding adds space inside cells, border-spacing sets gap when borders are separate. Make tables readable and professional. Change padding to 1rem or add a background color to th.

examples.title: Text input
examples.description: <input type="text"> collects a single line of text. Names, usernames, or short answers. Pair with <label> so users know what to type. Add a placeholder attribute like placeholder="Enter your name".

files.html:
<form onsubmit="event.preventDefault();">
  <label>Full name
    <input type="text" name="fullname" required>
  </label>
  <label>Student ID
    <input type="text" name="studentid" maxlength="10">
  </label>
</form>

examples.title: Radio buttons
examples.description: <input type="radio"> lets users pick exactly one option from a group. Gender, class session, or yes/no choices. All radios in a group need the same name attribute. Add a third radio option.

files.html:
<fieldset>
  <legend>Choose your session</legend>
  <label><input type="radio" name="session" value="morning" checked> Morning (8am)</label>
  <label><input type="radio" name="session" value="afternoon"> Afternoon (2pm)</label>
</fieldset>

examples.title: Checkboxes
examples.description: <input type="checkbox"> lets users select zero, one, or many options. Interests, skills, or agreeing to terms. Add another hobby checkbox.

files.html:
<fieldset>
  <legend>Your interests (pick any)</legend>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="interest" value="html" checked> HTML</label>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="interest" value="css"> CSS</label>
  <label><input type="checkbox" name="interest" value="js"> JavaScript</label>
</fieldset>

examples.title: The textarea element
examples.description: <textarea> collects multiple lines of text. Comments, essays, or feedback forms. Use rows and cols or CSS to control size. Increase rows to 6 and type a longer message.

files.html:
<form onsubmit="event.preventDefault();">
  <label>Your feedback
    <textarea name="feedback" rows="4" cols="40" placeholder="Write your thoughts here..."></textarea>
  </label>
</form>

examples.title: The select element
examples.description: <select> creates a dropdown menu; each <option> is one choice. Country, course, or any list with many choices. Add a fourth option to the dropdown.

files.html:
<form onsubmit="event.preventDefault();">
  <label>Favorite language
    <select name="language">
      <option value="">-- Choose one --</option>
      <option value="html">HTML</option>
      <option value="css">CSS</option>
      <option value="js">JavaScript</option>
    </select>
  </label>
</form>

examples.title: The button element
examples.description: <button type="button"> runs an action without submitting a form (e.g. show a message). Interactive controls inside or outside forms. Change the alert message text.

files.html:
Click the button to see a message:

type="button" does not submit a form.

examples.title: Submit button
examples.description: <button type="submit"> or <input type="submit"> sends form data to a server. End of a registration or contact form. Here we prevent real submission and show an alert instead. Change the button label to “Register now”.

files.html: Or use: <input type="submit" value="Send">

examples.title: Complete registration form
examples.description: Real forms combine text inputs, radio buttons, checkboxes, textarea, select, and submit in one <form>. Sign-up pages, surveys, or course registration. Add a required checkbox for “I agree to terms”.

files.html:
Course registration

Level
Topics

practice.description: Build a simple survey form with: text input (name), one radio group, one checkbox, a textarea, a select dropdown, and a submit button.

starter.html: Student survey

hints: Radio buttons in one group share the same name attribute
Use fieldset and legend to group related inputs
textarea does not use a closing slash — it needs </textarea>
